1. PRODUCT REQUIREMENTS DOCUMENT (PRD)
1.1 Product Vision
Build an intelligent multi-model chatbot that optimizes AI costs by 70-85%
through intelligent model routing while maintaining GPT-4 class quality. Users
can either let the system auto-route prompts to optimal models or manually
assign tasks to specific models using @mentions.
1.2 Core Features
Feature 1: Auto-Optimize Mode (Smart Routing)
User Story: As a user, I want the system to automatically choose the best
model(s) for my task so I don’t have to think about which AI to use.
Acceptance Criteria: - System analyzes prompt complexity (length, domain,
reasoning depth, code presence) - Routes to single model for simple tasks (<100
words, factual queries) - Routes to multiple models in parallel for complex tasks
(reasoning, code generation, multi-step) - Aggregates responses when multiple
models are used - Provides transparency: shows which model(s) were used and
estimated cost - Fallback chain: if primary model fails, auto-switch to backup
within 500ms
Cost Target: 50-85% reduction vs using GPT-4o for all queries
Feature 2: Manual Assignment Mode (@Mentions)
User Story: As a power user, I want to assign specific sub-tasks to specific
models using @mentions for precise control.
Acceptance Criteria: - Parse @model_name tags in prompts (e.g., @claude-
sonnet, @gpt-4o) - Support multiple @mentions in single prompt - Parallel

execution of tagged sub-tasks - Sequential dependency: @model2 after
@model1 completes - Context passing: output from @model1 feeds into
@model2 - Cost preview before execution: “This will cost ~$0.023 and use 3
models”
Example Prompts:
@claude-sonnet Write a Python function to calculate Fibonacci numbers w
ith memoization.
@gpt-4o-mini Write unit tests for the above function covering edge case
s.
@gemini-flash Generate documentation for this function.
Feature 3: Token & Cost Dashboard
User Story: As a user, I want to see real-time cost analytics so I can optimize my
spending.
Acceptance Criteria: - Real-time cost tracking per request, per model, per day -
Cost comparison: “You saved $12.40 this week using smart routing” - Token
usage breakdown: input vs output tokens - Budget alerts at 50%, 75%, 90% of
$49 credit - Usage forecasting: “At current rate, credits will last 18 days”
1.3 Success Metrics (KPIs)
Metric
Target
Measurement
Cost per 1K requests <$0.50
Via FastRouter analytics
Routing accuracy
>95%
Human evaluation on test set
Avg response time
<2s
95th percentile
User satisfaction
>4.5/5
Post-session survey
Credit utilization
30 days Time to deplete $49
Fallback rate
<2%
Auto-failover events
1.4 User Personas
1.
Casual User - Wants answers, doesn’t care about models. Uses Auto
mode.
2.
Developer - Needs code generation, testing, documentation. Uses
@mentions.
3.
Cost-Conscious Startup - Wants enterprise quality at startup
